title: [FOLDED → OOM-0036] Segfault / negative-refcount: over-decreffed `sys.path` entry in `PyType_IsSubtype` (`typeobject.c:2931`)
notes: RETIRED 2026-06-25 — folded into OOM-0036. rr reverse-execution of the repro (`sys.path[:] = S; __import__('H')` under the set_nomemory sweep) traced the over-decref'd victim (a 1-char `str` from S, held by sys.path) and found it is `list.append`-ed by the import machinery and freed when the grow fails under OOM: Py_DECREF(newitem==victim) @ Objects/listobject.c:531 <- `_PyList_AppendTakeRefListResize` <- `_CALL_LIST_APPEND` (generated_cases.c.h:3981). That is OOM-0036's list.append-under-MemoryError double-free (python/cpython#151818) -- the leftover stolen stackref is closed again during the unwind (frame.c:101). `PyType_IsSubtype`/`list_ass_slice` are only DETECTOR sites: the next loop iteration's `sys.path[:] = S` (list_ass_slice cleanup) Py_XDECREFs the already-freed element -> negref (the abort face), or import iterates the freed entry and PyType_IsSubtype reads a freed type -> segv (the release face). The ledger is step-for-step identical to OOM-0005's pkgutil trace -- both drive a path-string through the SAME import-machinery list.append. EARLIER (corrected): characterized as a distinct list_ass_slice over-decref; rr shows list_ass_slice is the detector, the producer is _CALL_LIST_APPEND. OOM-0033's keys (PyType_IsSubtype:2931, list_ass_slice/list_ass_subscript) were UNIQUE (no collision), so the fold simply drops them; a crash at those detector sites now surfaces as oomNEW -> rr-triage (almost always OOM-0036). RELEASE-CRASHING (upstream segv, like #151818): the `sys.path[:] = S; __import__` snippet is a good additional pure-stdlib release reproducer of #151818 -- repro.py kept in this tombstone dir; could be offered on the issue. Gist 249032e1 should be marked superseded (flagged to maintainer). Gen scripts skip status==folded. id NOT reused.
